name: Joy of Satan
description: Joy of Satan (JoS) is a controversial website and ideology founded in 2002 by Maxine Dietrich (pseudonym of Andrea Dietrich). It presents a unique form of Satanism that blends elements of Theistic Satanism, National Socialism, and UFO conspiracy theories. JoS rejects the traditional Judeo-Christian depiction of Satan as evil, instead portraying him as a benevolent creator god and liberator of humanity. The website promotes Satan as the true father of humanity, suppressed by a tyrannical and deceptive creator deity, often equated with the God of the Old Testament. JoS adherents believe that through meditation, ritual, and the study of occult knowledge, individuals can awaken their own divine potential and achieve spiritual enlightenment. JoS also espouses controversial views on race, promoting what they term 'spiritual racism' and claiming that certain races are inherently more spiritually advanced than others. This aspect of the ideology has drawn considerable criticism and accusations of Nazism and white supremacy. Mainstream religious scholars and Satanism experts generally reject JoS's interpretation of Satanism, viewing it as a distorted and dangerous misrepresentation of both historical Satanism and contemporary Satanic practice. Critics point to the group's association with hate speech and conspiracy theories as evidence of its harmful nature. The site's teachings deviate significantly from both traditional LaVeyan Satanism and other forms of Theistic Satanism. While JoS claims to offer gnosis and spiritual liberation, its controversial doctrines and association with extremist ideologies have led to widespread condemnation and concern.
subtitle: Controversial Satanic ideology with Nationalist leanings

claims.claim: Satan is a benevolent creator god.
claims.evidence: Interpretations of ancient mythology and personal gnosis.
claims.criticism: Contradicts traditional Judeo-Christian depictions of Satan and lacks historical or scholarly support within mainstream Satanic studies.

claims.claim: The God of the Old Testament is a tyrannical and deceptive creator.
claims.evidence: Interpretations of biblical texts and claims of alien intervention in human history.
claims.criticism: Represents a misinterpretation and demonization of Abrahamic religious figures, often fueled by antisemitic tropes.

claims.claim: Certain races are inherently more spiritually advanced.
claims.evidence: Dubious interpretations of genetic differences and alleged spiritual lineage.
claims.criticism: Based on pseudoscientific racism and lacks any scientific or ethical basis; promotes discrimination and white supremacist ideologies.

keyFigures.name: Maxine Dietrich (Andrea Dietrich)
keyFigures.role: Founder and primary author of Joy of Satan website

keyFigures.name: Anton LaVey
keyFigures.role: Founder of the Church of Satan; while JoS deviates from LaVeyan Satanism, it is often compared to it

keyFigures.name: George Michael (pseudonym)
keyFigures.role: Prominent author on the JoS website.

timeline.event: Joy of Satan website is founded by Maxine Dietrich.
Continued growth and spread of JoS ideology online, accompanied by controversy and accusations of hate speech.
